newsSinceJun6: Confirmed Russell 2000 inclusion effective June 26 (stock popped ~3% pre-market June 9 then reversed to -5.8% on the day - flow-driven, not fundamental). Form 4 disclosure of director Lawrence Finch selling 310,091 shares (~$8.5M); insiders have sold $17.2M over three months with zero buying. The late-May Kerrisdale short report (fair value $14, ~60% downside, argues largest end market is casino gaming not AI, revenue flat at $50-65M for years) continues to drive the unwind; no new earnings, contracts, or design wins since June 6.

sources.label: Investing.com - Everspin to join Russell 2000 index on June 26
TipRanks - Everspin director's multi-million dollar stock sale
Investing.com - Everspin tumbles after Kerrisdale short report
GuruFocus - MRAM rises pre-market on Russell 2000 inclusion

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved it down for crowding and post-rerating chase; today's -5.8% is the unwind that move anticipated, not new information. The sell-off modestly improves entry asymmetry, but that is offset by fresh insider-sale disclosure, Russell-inclusion passive ownership eroding the under-owned character, and Kerrisdale's unanswered claim that the real end market is casino gaming rather than edge AI/robotics.
verdicts.bull: Toggle/STT-MRAM is genuinely radiation-hard, instant-on persistent memory suited to industrial and robotic edge controllers, backed by a $40M defense contract; a ~20% drawdown from recent highs restores some asymmetry if the edge-AI design-win pipeline materializes.
verdicts.neutral: Still +127% YTD on flat $50-65M revenue; the de-rating is underway but incomplete, and there is no new fundamental datapoint since June 6 to re-underwrite the robotics thesis either way.
verdicts.bear: Kerrisdale's $14 fair value implies ~40% further downside, insiders have sold $17.2M with no buying, and if the dominant revenue base really is casino gaming the robotics-edge-memory framing is mostly narrative.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named robotics/edge-AI design win or Q2 revenue inflection that rebuts the flat-revenue short thesis; near-term, Russell 2000 inclusion flows into June 26.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued short-thesis-driven de-rating toward the mid-teens as AI-hype premium bleeds off a flat-revenue, insider-selling micro-cap.
